back_translation: When all this had ended, all the Israelites who were present went out to the cities of Judah and broke up the sacred pillars, cut down the Asherah poles, and tore down the high places and altars throughout Judah and Benjamin, as well as in Ephraim and Manasseh, until they had destroyed them all. Then all the Israelites returned to their cities, each to his own property.

Hezekiah reestablished the divisions of the priests and Levites, each division according to its duty, both priests and Levites, for burnt offerings and peace offerings, to minister, to give thanks, and to praise at the gates of the dwelling of the LORD.

The king also appointed his portion from his own possessions for the burnt offerings—the morning and evening burnt offerings, and the burnt offerings for the Sabbaths, New Moons, and appointed feasts—as written in the Law of the LORD.
He commanded the people living in Jerusalem to give the portion due to the priests and Levites, so that they could devote themselves to the Law of the LORD.
As soon as the command spread, the Israelites generously gave the firstfruits of grain, new wine, oil, honey, and all the produce of the field, and brought in abundantly the tithe of everything.

The Israelites and people of Judah who lived in the cities of Judah also brought the tithe of their herds and flocks, and the tithe of the holy things consecrated to the LORD their God, and laid them in heaps.
In the third month they began to build up the heaps, and finished them in the seventh month.
When Hezekiah and the officials came and saw the heaps, they blessed the LORD and His people Israel.
Hezekiah questioned the priests and the Levites about the heaps.

And Azariah the chief priest, of the household of Zadok, answered him, “Since the people began to bring their contributions into the house of the LORD, we have eaten and had enough and plenty left over, because the LORD has blessed His people; this great abundance is what is left.”
Then Hezekiah commanded them to prepare storerooms in the house of the LORD, and they prepared them.
They faithfully brought in the contributions, the tithes, and the dedicated gifts. Conaniah the Levite was the chief officer over them, and his brother Shimei was second.

Jehiel, Azaziah, Nahath, Asahel, Jerimoth, Jozabad, Eliel, Ismachiah, Mahath, and Benaiah were overseers under Conaniah and his brother Shimei, by appointment of King Hezekiah and of Azariah the chief officer of the house of God.
Kore son of Imnah the Levite, keeper of the East Gate, was in charge of the freewill offerings given to God, to distribute the contributions of the LORD and the most holy things.
Under his charge were Eden, Miniamin, Jeshua, Shemaiah, Amariah, and Shecaniah in the cities of the priests, to distribute faithfully to their brothers by divisions, to great and small alike.

In addition, they distributed to the males registered by genealogy from three years old and up—all who entered the house of the LORD for their daily duties, for the service of their offices according to their divisions.
The enrollment of the priests was by their fathers' houses, and the Levites from twenty years old and up, according to their duties and divisions.
They were enrolled together with all their little ones, wives, sons, and daughters—the whole assembly—for they were faithful in keeping themselves holy.

As for the sons of Aaron, the priests who lived in the farmlands around their cities, or in any city, men designated by name in each city distributed portions to every male among the priests and to everyone enrolled among the Levites.
Hezekiah did this throughout all Judah. He did what was good and right and faithful before the LORD his God.
In every work that he began, in the service of the house of God, and in the law and the commandments, to seek his God, he acted with all his heart, and he prospered.
